hero.subtitle: Jobs, training, entrepreneurship, childcare, and family support — all in your community.
hero.body: OASIS 360 connects San Juan families with the resources they need to move forward. From finding a quality job to starting your own business or getting support for your family — we're here.

home.whatIsText: OASIS 360 is a comprehensive initiative based in Villa Prades, San Juan, that brings together multiple organizations with a single purpose: creating real pathways to economic mobility for families in ZIP codes 00923 and 00924. You're not here to fill out forms — you're here to build your future with a team that walks beside you every step.
home.hubText: In the heart of Villa Prades, there's a space where education, employment, health, and entrepreneurship come together under one roof. The OASIS Hub isn't a government office — it's your space. Here your family finds school, you find training, and your neighbor finds support for their business.
home.ctaTitle: Ready to take the first step?
home.ctaSubtitle: No matter where you are today — there's a path for you. Let's talk.

mejorEmpleo.name: Better Jobs for Me
mejorEmpleo.tagline: Personalized training and quality job placement to transform your career.
mejorEmpleo.heroTagline: Changing the story so families can thrive
mejorEmpleo.heroBody: This program connects you with quality jobs through personalized training, mentorship, and job placement. It's not a generic course — it's a plan designed for you, your goals, and your reality.
mejorEmpleo.challenge: In ZIP codes 00923 and 00924, the average annual income is just $19,318 — 20% below the Puerto Rico average. Only 58% of adults have a high school diploma and just 9% have a college education. The combination of high population density, limited access to economic resources, and a local economy dominated by low-wage sectors creates a structural disadvantage that limits access to quality jobs.
mejorEmpleo.step1Desc: We learn about your interests, skills, and goals. Together we identify high-demand sectors: healthcare, technology, construction, and care services.
mejorEmpleo.step2Desc: Training programs designed in partnership with real employers and workforce partners. Not theory — learning by doing.
mejorEmpleo.step3Desc: A mentor guides you, prepares you for interviews, and connects you directly with partner employers looking for talent like you.
mejorEmpleo.step4Desc: We place you in a quality job ($19/hour or more full-time) and stay with you afterward to make sure you stay and grow.
services: Personalized work plans based on your goals
Custom training programs with real employers
Connection to complementary services: childcare, transportation, housing
mejorEmpleo.audience: Residents of ZIP codes 00923 and 00924 seeking quality employment or better job opportunities.
mejorEmpleo.ctaTitle: Ready to find a better job?
mejorEmpleo.ctaBody: Your next step starts with a conversation. Let's talk about your goals and how we can help you reach them.

emprendimiento.name: Entrepreneurship E3
emprendimiento.tagline: From your idea to growing your business — we're with you at every stage.
emprendimiento.heroBody: E3 is a comprehensive strategy to transform the business ecosystem in your community. Whether you have an idea, an informal business, or a business you want to grow — there's a place for you.
emprendimiento.challenge: Your community has plenty of talent — what's missing are accessible pathways. An estimated 25% of financial activity is informal, because many residents fear that formalizing their business will cost them housing subsidies and other benefits. 88% of business owners in Puerto Rico report difficulty obtaining permits. And only 38% of men and 20% of women have basic financial literacy. We tackle those barriers head-on.
emprendimiento.step1Desc: From idea to action. Business concept development, registration, initial permits. An intensive 8-week course that gives you the fundamental tools, culminating in a demo day with ecosystem stakeholders.
emprendimiento.step2Desc: Your business exists — now we stabilize it. Personalized technical assistance, financial literacy, access to capital, and legal, marketing, and operational guidance.
emprendimiento.step3Desc: Ready to grow. Strategic planning, product diversification, export plans, and access to networks and capital to scale your business and create jobs.
emprendimiento.step4Desc: You never walk alone. Permanent support network among entrepreneurs, ongoing mentorship, advanced opportunities, and participation in the Community Business Association.
emprendimiento.audience: Entrepreneurs and aspiring business owners in ZIP codes 00923/00924.
emprendimiento.ctaBody: It doesn't matter if it's on a napkin or you already have customers. Let's talk about taking it to the next level.

comunidadDeCuidado.name: Caring Community
comunidadDeCuidado.tagline: Childcare, eldercare, and afterschool programs so you can work with peace of mind.
comunidadDeCuidado.heroTagline: So you can work with peace of mind
comunidadDeCuidado.heroBody: We know you can't take a job if there's nobody to watch your kids. And you can't take a night shift if there's no afterschool program. This initiative removes the caregiving barriers standing in your way.
comunidadDeCuidado.challenge: 71% of residents with children in ZIP codes 00923 and 00924 say the lack of afterschool and childcare services is a significant barrier to employment. 29% of the population can't transition to full-time work because of care costs. For eldercare, waiting lists are long and existing centers have limited capacity. Despite subsidies like care vouchers, their use remains limited due to lack of awareness, bureaucratic processes, and insufficient supply of certified providers.

challengeStats.label: say care shortage is a barrier to work
can't work full-time due to care costs

comunidadDeCuidado.step1Desc: We assess your family situation to understand exactly what type of care you need — childcare, afterschool, eldercare, or a combination.
comunidadDeCuidado.step2Desc: We connect you with certified providers and help you enroll in existing care voucher programs.
comunidadDeCuidado.step3Desc: We're creating new care spaces in the community, including overnight and weekend options that didn't exist before.
comunidadDeCuidado.step4Desc: We stay with you to make sure the care solution works for your family long-term.
services: New care businesses created in the community (75 jobs)
comunidadDeCuidado.audience: Families needing access to childcare, elder care, or after-school programs in order to work.
comunidadDeCuidado.ctaBody: We'll connect you with childcare options, afterschool programs, and eldercare support in your area.

navegadorFamiliar.name: Family Navigator
navegadorFamiliar.tagline: Whole-family support: health, housing, employment, education, and more, with a dedicated navigator.
navegadorFamiliar.heroTagline: Your family at the center of everything
navegadorFamiliar.heroBody: A Family Navigator isn't a social worker. It's someone who walks with you. Who knows the resources, understands your reality, and helps you create a real plan for your family — employment, housing, health, education, childcare, and more. All connected. All in one place.
navegadorFamiliar.challenge: Over 50% of families in ZIP codes 00923 and 00924 live below the poverty line. Nearly 30% of households are led by single mothers. There are decades of distrust toward institutional systems because services have been fragmented, bureaucratic, and disconnected from each other. We're changing that.
navegadorFamiliar.step1Desc: We meet you where you are. We work in 7 schools in your area (grades K–3) to identify families that would benefit from our support.
navegadorFamiliar.step2Desc: We look at 8 key dimensions of family well-being: health, education, income, employment, housing, transportation, childcare, and food security. Not to judge — to understand how to help.
navegadorFamiliar.step3Desc: Together we create an annual plan with specific goals for the adults and children in your family. You decide the priorities.
navegadorFamiliar.step4Desc: Your Navigator walks with you continuously: removing barriers, connecting you to services, and making sure no one falls behind.
navegadorFamiliar.serviceLevel1Desc: A dedicated Family Navigator who works with you and your family directly. Co-created plans, continuous monitoring, and comprehensive support year-round.
navegadorFamiliar.serviceLevel2Desc: No appointment needed. When you need something specific — help with a form, a referral, guidance about a service — just reach out and we'll assist you.
services: Referrals to employment, training, childcare, health, and housing services
Individualized case management or on-demand navigation
navegadorFamiliar.audience: Families in ZIP codes 00923/00924 needing comprehensive support to overcome barriers to stability.
navegadorFamiliar.ctaBody: A Family Navigator is ready to listen. The first step is a conversation.

empoderados.name: Empowered for Employment (E2)
empoderados.tagline: Getting a job shouldn't mean losing your benefits. We're changing the rules.
empoderados.heroTagline: Working shouldn't mean losing what you have
empoderados.heroBody: Have you ever thought about looking for work but were held back by fear of losing your benefits? You're not alone. 60% of non-working families say the same thing. This initiative exists to change that — with real information, pilot programs, and a community voice demanding better policies.
empoderados.partner: IDJ (Instituto del Desarrollo de la Juventud)
empoderados.challenge: They call it a "cliff" because that's exactly what it feels like. When your income goes up a little — sometimes just $100 more a month — you can lose benefits worth thousands of dollars a year: food assistance, housing, childcare subsidies. That's not fair. And it discourages talented people who want to work. We're researching how this works, training government case managers themselves, and testing real solutions.
empoderados.step1Desc: In-depth study of how families lose benefits when they start working — interviews, surveys, focus groups, and published reports.
empoderados.step2Desc: Residents of ZIP codes 00923 and 00924 designing public policy from their lived experience. Your voice matters. Your story changes the rules.
empoderados.step3Desc: Pilot program that trains government eligibility workers (starting with TANF) to tell you the truth: starting to work doesn't always mean losing everything at once.
empoderados.step4Desc: Direct transfer pilot program: $4,200 in payments every 3 months over 18 months to 75 families. We're measuring whether this helps families make the leap to employment without falling off the cliff.
services: Understanding of how employment affects your benefits
empoderados.audience: Families fearing benefit loss when entering employment and those seeking to change the policies creating these barriers.
empoderados.ctaBody: If you're afraid to work because you don't want to lose your benefits, let's talk. There are more options than you think.

hub.title: The OASIS Hub
hub.subtitle: Where everything connects
hub.description: The OASIS Hub is a multifunctional center in Villa Prades, San Juan, that integrates education, health, employment, entrepreneurship, and youth programs under one roof. More than a building, it's an ecosystem where families find what they need without running all over the city.
hub.educationDesc: Vimenti School offers quality education from kindergarten through 12th grade.
hub.workforceDesc: Over 300 individuals trained annually in market-demanded skills.
hub.healthcareDesc: Vidalús Community Health Clinic serves over 4,000 patients annually.
hub.entrepreneurshipDesc: Project Makers supports 100+ entrepreneurs with incubation and acceleration.
hub.youthDesc: Over 550 children and youth participate in after-school programs.
hub.address: Villa Prades, San Juan, Puerto Rico

partners.title: Our Partners
partners.subtitle: OASIS 360 isn't a single organization — it's a coalition. Each partner brings unique expertise so your family has access to the best in employment, education, entrepreneurship, care, and public policy.
partners.vimentiDesc: Family Navigator and Better Jobs for Me. K–12 school and two-generation model supporting the whole family.
partners.projectMakersDesc: Community business incubator and accelerator. From the initial idea to expansion.
partners.idjDesc: Research, public policy, and the Empowered for Employment program. Changing the rules so that working doesn't hurt you.
partners.goodwillDesc: Adult education, credentials, and workforce referrals.
partners.psiDesc: Overall coordination, governance, Caring Community, and connecting capital with communities.
partners.govPartners: Departamento de la Familia, Departamento de Educación, Departamento de Vivienda, Departamento de Salud, Municipio de San Juan, Boys and Girls Club, Transita
partners.ctaText: Join our network of organizations committed to economic mobility in San Juan.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: We're here to help. Whether you're looking for a job, want to start a business, need childcare, or support for your family — reach out and we'll connect you with the right resource.
contact.successText: We received your message. Someone from our team will be in touch soon.

footer.attribution: This initiative is funded by the U.S. Economic Development Administration (EDA) through the RECOMPETE Plan — Grant ED24HDQ0G0516.

ctas.helpBanner: Need help? We're here for you.
